steps.title: Factor the difference
steps.content:
Use the difference of squares.
$$x^2-y^2=(x-y)(x+y)$$
So the first equation becomes
$$(x-y)(x+y)=39$$

steps.title: Use the given difference
steps.content:
The second equation gives $x-y=3$. Substitute that into the factored equation.
$$3(x+y)=39$$
$$x+y=13$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the system and read the value of $x+y$ from the intersection. The answer is C.
